data.title: Enforce plugin boundary: relocate dev-only artifacts and add allowlist pre-commit hook
data.description:
## Context
DSO plugin developers — and AI agents acting as developers — currently place dev-team-targeted artifacts (ADRs, design docs, research findings, migration guides, dev tests) inside plugins/dso/, causing them to be packaged and shipped into every host project's plugin cache alongside user-facing content. End users receive contributor noise; developers have no enforced boundary distinguishing shipped from local-only content. Without automation, agents writing new docs or tests will continue violating this boundary silently. Note: dso-q0lo addresses volume of LLM-generated documentation; this epic addresses placement only. Coordinate with dso-q0lo if active concurrently to avoid conflicting edits to the doc-writer agent definition and CLAUDE.md doc-placement rules.

## Success Criteria
- All dev-team-targeted files relocated to root equivalents (docs/adr/, docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/reference/, docs/, .claude/docs/, tests/skills/) — cascade-replan-protocol.md is an exception: it moves to plugins/dso/skills/sprint/docs/
- A pre-commit hook exists with an explicit allowlist of permitted path patterns inside plugins/dso/ and blocks any commit adding a file outside that allowlist
- A CLAUDE.md rule defines the plugin boundary with concrete examples of shipped vs. local-only artifact categories; verified by running grep for each relocated path prefix in CLAUDE.md returning zero matches
- The doc-writer agent definition is updated to include docs/designs/, docs/findings/, and docs/archive/ as valid output locations for dev-team artifact types, using positive enumeration framing with an explicit prohibition against writing to plugins/dso/
- Running a path-pattern scan against ~/.claude/plugins/cache/digital-service-orchestra/ for dev-only path patterns (adr/, /designs/, /findings/, /archive/, migration) returns zero matches on a fresh install
- A test that attempts to commit a file at plugins/dso/docs/adr/test-adr.md confirms the pre-commit hook exits non-zero with stderr output naming the correct root-equivalent path (docs/adr/); runnable within the sprint session as part of the test suite
- Running grep for all relocated path prefixes across plugins/dso/skills/ and plugins/dso/ returns zero results; update-docs/SKILL.md requires no path update (dispatches doc-writer by agent name only); ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md updated to write to docs/designs/<uuid>/ with an explicit prohibition against writing to plugins/dso/

## Scenario Analysis
- Allowlist hook fails open on parse error (high, runtime): If the hook encounters a read/parse error on the allowlist file, it may default to exit 0, silently permitting all files including dev-only artifacts.
- Stale internal references cause skill execution failures (high, runtime): Missed references to relocated files — particularly dynamically constructed paths — manifest as runtime failures when that code path executes.
- Pre-commit hook not executable after clone (high, deployment): Git silently skips non-executable hook scripts; enforcement appears in place but nothing is blocked.
- Allowlist too broad: prefix overlap (high, configuration): Prefix-based glob patterns may inadvertently permit dev-only files at similar paths, defeating the boundary.
- Allowlist too narrow: new shipped-content paths blocked (medium, configuration): New legitimate plugin paths not on the allowlist cause repeated commit failures until manually updated.

## Dependencies
None (coordinate with dso-q0lo if active concurrently)

## Approach
Relocate all misplaced files to their root equivalents, add an allowlist-based pre-commit hook matching the pattern of existing hooks in plugins/dso/hooks/, pair with CLAUDE.md and doc-writer agent updates using positive enumeration + NEVER-statement constraint framing. Update all internal skill and prompt references.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 5
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: agent definitions

### Prompt Alignment Findings
- Matched signal: agent definitions (SC4 doc-writer update; SC7 ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md update)
- Prior-art patterns: two-part constraint (positive enumeration + NEVER-statement) outperforms prohibition-only in long-context sessions; output constraints belong in Role/Constraints section at top of agent prompt to avoid positional bias failure mode
- Behavioral review: bot-psychologist dispatch failed — skipped per graceful degradation
- Recommendations: (1) doc-writer update must use positively-framed constraint alongside schema table to prevent agents defaulting to Tier 4 (docs/adr/) for design docs; (2) ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md must include both positive path and explicit plugins/dso/ prohibition
